frameworks.name: holistic Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: unified Bayesian observer combining efficient encoding with categorical novelty inference

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: sensory encoding adapts to predicted future stimulus distribution

frameworks.name: novelty detection
frameworks.one_line: categorical inference about whether stimulus matches adaptor distribution

core_question: What is the functional purpose of perceptual adaptation, and can efficient coding and novelty detection be unified within a single Bayesian model?
key_assumptions: sensory encoding adapts to maximize efficiency under fixed resource limits
observer performs categorical inference about novelty relative to adaptor distribution
future stimulus distribution is predicted from previous input statistics
manipulations: presence of single adaptor distribution prior to test stimulus
varying adaptor configurations (single and arbitrary adaptors)
key_findings: model accounts for previously reported perceptual aftereffects in estimation tasks
model explains characteristic repulsive biases after adaptation to a single adaptor
model explains changes in estimation variance following adaptation
model predicts aftereffects for arbitrary adaptors
interpretations: efficient coding and novelty detection jointly account for adaptation effects
perceptual adaptation can be understood as holistic Bayesian inference
